description: General laborers, unskilled workers, and itinerant roles.
jobs: Burgher
A charter-holding townsman with the right to trade, vote, and own property within the walls.
Peasant
A bent-backed toiler working the lord's fields from dawn to dusk, bound by custom.
Serf
A land-chained laborer tilling soil they can never leave, owing harvest shares to the lord.
Villein
A strip-farming tenant burdened with heavy obligations to the manor estate.
Charcoal Burner
A soot-blackened hermit tending smoldering wood piles deep in the forest.
Net Mender
A dock-sitting worker stitching torn mesh back together with needle and tarred twine.
Caravan Master
A route-planning leader hiring guards and keeping a merchant caravan moving on schedule.
Teamster
A whip-cracking driver handling a team of draft horses hauling heavy loads by wagon.
Carter
A creaking-axle driver delivering goods from warehouse to market and back again.
Ferryman
A pole-pushing boatman carrying passengers across the river for a coin per crossing.
Boatman
A rope-calloused oarsman rowing passengers and cargo along rivers and canals for a fare.
Dock Worker
A barrel-hoisting laborer loading and unloading ships at the harbor quay.
Warehouse Keeper
A ledger-toting guardian of stored goods, watching for theft and vermin alike.
Factor
A distant-port agent managing a merchant's buying and selling affairs abroad.
Porter
A strap-shouldered hauler carrying heavy trunks and packages through streets and stairways.
Coachman
A rain-soaked driver sitting atop the coach box, steering horse teams through mud to the destination.
Messenger
A dust-cloaked rider carrying sealed letters and verbal orders between courts and camps.
Runner
A fleet-footed youth sprinting through city streets with urgent parcels and short messages.
Gentleman Usher
A white-gloved escort guiding guests into formal chambers and managing ceremonial seating.
Demon Hunter
A sulfur-tracking slayer banishing fiends with silver blade and whispered rite.
Vampire Hunter
A stake-and-garlic stalker of the undead, cornering them in their daytime lairs.
Monster Hunter
A trap-laden bounty taker arriving with swords, silver, and hard-won knowledge of beasts.
Dragon Slayer
A fireproof-salved warrior seeking great wyrms in their lairs, lance couched and ready.
Ward Keeper
A glyph-maintaining sentinel keeping dark forces from crossing a protected threshold.
Time Keeper
A glass-turning attendant monitoring hourglasses and sundials to mark the passing hours.
Gambler
A dice-rattling risk-taker living on luck and bluff, playing cards for their daily bread.
Witch Hunter
An iron-bearing investigator testing suspects for forbidden magic with prayer and ordeal.
Town Crier
A bell-ringing voice shouting the day's news, laws, and royal decrees on street corners.
Lamp Lighter
A long-pole-carrying walker lighting oil lamps one by one as dusk settles on the streets.
Night Soil Man
A rag-masked hauler emptying cesspits under cover of darkness and carting waste out of town.
Gong Farmer
A stench-soaked digger descending into reeking privies to shovel them clean.
Rat Catcher
A terrier-followed hunter chasing rats with traps and poison through cellars and sewers.
Pest Controller
A smoke-and-powder specialist clearing infestations of mice, beetles, and vermin from homes.
Gravedigger
A spade-leaning loner breaking earth to cut fresh graves in the churchyard soil.
Undertaker
A somber-suited preparer washing the dead, building coffins, and arranging proper burials.
Mortician
A herb-scented embalmer preserving corpses with oils and spices for viewing and transport.
Sin Eater
A bread-consuming outcast ritually absorbing the dead person's sins over the open coffin.
Professional Mourner
A black-veiled wailer beating their chest at funerals, paid to amplify the grief.
Wanderer
A pack-carrying drifter with no fixed home, living by odd jobs and roadside charity.
Beggar
A cup-rattling figure sitting at crossroads, asking passersby for a coin to survive.
Vagrant
A barn-sleeping shadow moving on before anyone asks too many questions.
Leper
A bell-ringing outcast keeping distance from all others, cast beyond the walls by disease.
Madman
A wild-haired wanderer raving in the streets, pitied or feared by everyone who passes.
Dwarf
A short-statured individual often displayed as a novelty at court, yet sharp of mind.
Giant
A towering figure looming over common folk, sometimes hired as spectacle or living wall.
Freak
A road-show captive gawked at for unusual traits, displayed for coin in traveling tents.
Chimney Sweep
A soot-caked climber squeezing into choked flues with brush and scraper.
Rag Picker
A refuse-heap comber salvaging rags, bones, and scrap metal to resell for pennies.
Dung Collector
A shovel-wielding gatherer of street and pen manure, selling it as fertilizer to farmers.
Soap Boiler
A fat-rendering stirrer of lye-and-tallow cauldrons, producing blocks of cleaning soap.
Lime Burner
A kiln-tending worker firing limestone until it crumbles into quicklime for mortar.
Treasure Hunter
A map-following digger chasing rumors of buried gold and lost artifacts.
Relic Hunter
A tomb-delving seeker of holy relics, selling saint's bones to churches and collectors.
Guide
A trail-wise local leading travelers safely through unfamiliar forests and mountain passes.
Mountain Climber
A rope-and-piton scaler of peaks and cliff faces, driven by raw nerve.
Cave Explorer
A torchlit crawler mapping underground passages, squeezing through what lies below.
Swamp Guide
A mud-booted navigator steering travelers past treacherous bogs and hidden quicksand.
Desert Nomad
A sun-wrapped roamer of arid wastes, surviving on cached water and scarce game.
Well Digger
A shaft-sinking laborer digging by hand and shoring with stone until clean water rises.
Miner
A dust-lunged tunneler swinging a pickaxe underground, chipping ore from the rock face.
Quarryman
A wedge-driving splitter of great stone blocks from open quarry faces.
Prospector
A pan-swirling loner chipping at rock faces and hunting for veins of gold or silver.
Doomsayer
A wild-eyed prophet shouting warnings of plague, war, and divine wrath on every corner.
Puzzle Solver
A lock-picking thinker cracking ancient riddles and mechanical traps for adventuring parties.
Riddle Master
A sly-grinning inventor of brain-twisting riddles, challenging travelers for a prize.
Game Master
A booth-running operator of games of chance and skill at fairs, setting rules and awarding prizes.
Funeral Director
A black-suited arranger of every burial detail, from casket to graveside procession.
Matchmaker
A family-studying broker of advantageous marriages, weighing fortunes and temperaments.
Go-Between
A neutral carrier of offers and threats between parties who refuse to meet face to face.
Message Runner
A sweat-soaked sprinter racing on foot between towns with urgent letters and dispatches.
Courier
A saddlebag-laden rider delivering sealed packages across long distances by horse.
Post Rider
A relay-galloping carrier of the royal mail, changing horses at every station.
Signal Fire Keeper
A hilltop sentinel watching the horizon and lighting beacon fires to relay warnings.
Bell Ringer
A rope-pulling tower worker swinging heavy church bells that mark hours and sound alarms.
Bridge Keeper
A toll-collecting guardian of a bridge crossing, raising the span when ships pass.
Ferry Operator
A cable-hauling boatman pulling a flat ferry back and forth for paying passengers.
River Guide
A current-reading helmsman steering rafts through rapids and shallow channels.
Mountain Guide
A altitude-weathered leader choosing safe routes through snow, scree, and treacherous rock.
Forest Guide
A soft-stepping woodsman navigating dense trails and keeping travelers clear of predators.
Wilderness Survivor
A lean-muscled loner living off the land for weeks, building shelters from what the forest gives.
Demon Fighter
A ward-tattooed warrior charging into battle against fiends with blessed steel raised.
Ghost Hunter
A salt-circle-drawing investigator of hauntings, armed with cold-iron bells and binding chants.
Vampire Slayer
A stake-driving hunter cornering blood-drinkers in coffin and crypt by torchlight.
Werewolf Hunter
A silver-tipped-arrow carrier tracking shapeshifters by the lunar cycle and wolfsbane scent.
Monster Slayer
A scar-covered mercenary taking on any beast for the right price, from basilisk to manticore.
Dragon Hunter
A scorched-shield bearer venturing into wyrm lairs slathered in fireproof salve.
Giant Killer
A cunning little fighter bringing down towering foes with tripwires and well-aimed stones.
Troll Slayer
A torch-and-axe fighter hacking regenerating trolls apart and burning the pieces.
Goblin Exterminator
A smoke-bomb-throwing clearer of goblin warrens, working by blade and terrier dog.
Pest Eliminator
A venom-resistant specialist ridding farms of giant rats, snakes, and other dangerous pests.
Exterminator
A poison-and-trap layer smoking out infestations of vermin from buildings and sewers.
Mimic
A face-shifting deceiver copying voices and gaits so well they can pass as someone else.
Travel Guide
A street-wise local showing visitors the notable sights, shortcuts, and safe taverns.
Tour Leader
A story-narrating walker leading groups through historic ruins and city landmarks.
Pilgrimage Guide
A staff-carrying leader of the faithful along ancient holy routes to distant shrines.
Caravan Leader
A pace-setting veteran picking campsites for a merchant caravan crossing hostile land.
Expedition Leader
A supply-counting planner of routes and contingencies for ventures into uncharted territory.
Safari Guide
A rifle-bearing leader of armed parties into wild country to hunt rare and dangerous game.
Wagon Driver
A rein-gripping teamster handling a loaded wagon over rutted country roads.
Mule Driver
A goad-wielding handler leading strings of stubborn mules along narrow mountain trails.
Ox Driver
A patient prodder of slow oxen, hauling the heaviest loads at a plodding walking pace.
Pit Digger
A sweat-drenched excavator of deep holes for foundations, traps, and storage cellars.
Organ Grinder
A crank-turning street musician with a hand organ and a coin-collecting monkey on a chain.
Oddity Collector
A cabinet-filling seeker of strange artifacts and freakish specimens from distant lands.
Relic Collector
A shrine-haunting buyer of saint's bones, holy fragments, and blessed objects.
Rag and Bone Man
A handcart-pushing buyer of rags, bones, and household scrap from alley to alley.
Scrap Collector
A bent-nail-gathering salvager selling recovered metal scraps to smiths and smelters.
Waste Collector
A cart-loading hauler of refuse, dragging it beyond the city walls for dumping.
Street Cleaner
A wide-broom-wielding sweeper clearing muck and dung from cobblestone streets.
Chimney Cleaner
A brush-dragging worker scraping layers of creosote and soot from chimney flues.
Window Cleaner
A ladder-perched wiper of grime from glass panes with rags and vinegar.
Floor Scrubber
A knees-down laborer scouring stone and wood floors with sand and lye.
Fabric Bleacher
A field-spreading worker treating cloth with lye and sunlight to whiten the fibers.
Cloth Presser
A heavy-iron-wielding smoother running heated presses across finished fabric.
Hearth Keeper
A pre-dawn stoker of the household fire, banking it at night so it never goes cold.
